rulings.Case Title: ANN GARRISON VS. ONE WEST BANK et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 15, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT KENNINGTON LIMITED, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. rulings.Case Title: JUDITH ROGERS VS. RUBIN SALEM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 15, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT RUBIN SALEM, ZAHIRA SALEM, AND MUHAMMED SALEM'S Motion For Protective Order. GRANTED. THE COURT ADOPTS DEFENDANTS' ORDER, EXCEPT REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS DENIED, EACH SIDE ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JUDITH ROGERS VS. RUBIN SALEM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Pltf'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories, Requests For Admission And Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 15, 2010, line 2, PLAINTIFFS JUDITH ROGERS, DELCIA CARTER Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff's First Set Of Special Interrogatories, Requests For Admission And Form Interrogatories. DENIED. PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT ENTITLED TO DISCOVERY THAT DOES NOT RELATE TO THEIR INDIVIDUAL CLAIMS AND EXCEEDS THE PROPER SCOPE OF DISCOVERY. THIS ACTION IS NOT PLED AS A CLASS ACTION. THE SAN FRANCISCO MINIMUM WAGE ORDINANCE, WHICH REFERENCES A "PERSON...ACTING ON BEHALF OF THE PUBLIC AS PROVIDED FOR UNDER APPLICABLE STATE LAW" IS SIMILAR TO THE UNFAIR COMPETITION LAW. THE ORDINANCE IS DISSIMILAR TO THE LABOR CODE PRIVATE ATTORNEYS GENERAL ACT OF 2004 WHICH ALLOWS AN AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEE TO BRING A CIVIL ACTION PERSONALLY AND ON BEHALF OF OTHER EMPLOYEES TO RECOVER CIVIL PENALTIES, WITH 75 PERCENT GOING TO THE LABOR AND WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT AGENCY AND 25 PERCENT TO THE AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 15, 2010, line 5, PLAINTIFF KENNY & MARKOWITZ, LLP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. OFF CALENDAR, DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION FILED ON SEPTEMBER 22, 2010. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN SMITH VS. RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Writ Of Mandamus Pursuant To Code Of Covil Procedure 1094.5 And Administrative Record
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 15, 2010, line 10, PETITIONER STEPHEN SMITH'S Motion For Writ Of Mandamus Pursuant To Code Of Covil Procedure 1094.5 And Administrative Record. MOTION DENIED. THE COURT HAS USED ITS INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT AND REVIEWED THE ENTIRE RECORD, AND FINDS THE DECISION OF THE HEARING OFFICER IS BASED ON SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE AND SUPPORTED BY THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE. DR. LEONARD'S FINDINGS THAT PETITIONER HAS NO DISABILITY AND NO PRECLUSIONS BASED UPON A REVIEW OF THE MEDICAL RECORDS, PHYSICAL EXAMINATIONS, AND REVIEW OF A SUB ROSA VIDEOTAPE ARE FAR MORE PERSUASIVE THEN DR. BLAU'S RECOMMENDATION FOR WORK RESTRICTIONS ON A PROPHYLACTIC BASIS ONLY. THE ERRORS IN DR. LEONARD'S REPORT DO NOT PERTAIN TO THE CARDIAC ISSUE AND DO NOT DISCREDIT HIS MEDICAL OPINION THAT PETITIONER IS NOT INCAPACITATED FROM PERFORMING HIS DUTIES. THE MEDICAL RECORDS AND TEST RESULTS WEIGH HEAVILY IN SUPPORT OF THE HEARING OFFICER'S DECISION. =(302/CWW)
